turns.text: Thanks for calling Smith Plumbing, this is Katie, how can I help you?
Yeah hi, I've got a drain that's been completely backed up for two days now and I'm losing my mind over here.
Oh no, two days with a backed-up drain — that's really frustrating, I'm sorry you're dealing with that.
Yeah it's the kitchen sink and there's standing water just sitting there, it smells horrible. I already tried Drano twice and nothing.
Standing water in the kitchen sink even after Drano — sounds like it's a pretty serious clog down in the line.
I mean I can't even use my kitchen at this point. Can someone come out or what?
Absolutely, let me check what we've got open this week.
The sooner the better, honestly.
Sure thing — I've got Thursday at 10 AM or Friday at 2 PM, which works better for you?
Thursday. Definitely Thursday, I can't wait til Friday.
Thursday at 10 AM it is. Can I get your name?
Mike Dalton.
And a good phone number for you, Mike?
Got it. So Mike, we've got you down for Thursday at 10 AM for that clogged kitchen drain — our tech will give you a call about 30 minutes before they head out.
Alright, thank you. Sorry for being short, it's just been a nightmare.
No need to apologize at all, we'll get it taken care of for you. Have a good one, Mike!
Good afternoon, Smith Plumbing, this is Katie speaking.
Oh hello dear. Yes, I'm calling because my, um, the drain in my bathroom — the bathtub — it's not going down right.
Your bathtub drain isn't draining properly — how long has that been going on?
Well let me think now... I noticed it maybe, oh, last week? It's been slow. And now it's just sitting there, the water. My daughter said I should call a plumber.
Your daughter gave you good advice — a slow drain that stops completely usually means there's a buildup in the pipe.
Yes, yes. So can someone come and look at it? I'm home most days, you know, so anytime really.
Let me check what we have available... I'm so sorry, but this week and next week are actually completely booked up right now.
Oh. Oh dear. So there's nothing at all?
I know, I'm sorry about that. I can put you on our waitlist and if anything opens up we'll call you right away — or I can book the first opening we have, which is the week of the 21st.
Well... I suppose I can wait. It's not an emergency or anything, just a nuisance. Put me down for whatever you have that week, dear.
Sure thing, I'll put you on the waitlist and also book you for Monday the 21st at 9 AM — can I get your name?
It's Dorothy. Dorothy Hensley. H-E-N-S-L-E-Y.
Thank you, Dorothy. And what's the best number to reach you?
Let me think... it's 817-555-0243. That's my home phone.
Perfect. So Dorothy, you're on our waitlist and also booked for Monday the 21st at 9 AM for that bathtub drain — we'll call you if anything opens up sooner.
That sounds just fine, thank you so much for your help, dear.
You're so welcome, Dorothy. We'll take good care of you. Have a lovely day!
Smith Plumbing, this is Katie, how can I help?
Yes hello, I have problem with the, uh, the water in the kitchen. It no go down.
The water in your kitchen sink isn't going down — sounds like you've got a clogged drain.
Yes, yes, clogged. The water stay there, very dirty. Maybe one day now.
Okay, so the water's been sitting there since yesterday — we can definitely help with that.
Good, good. When you can come?
Let me check for you — how about Wednesday at 1 PM?
Wednesday... one o'clock. Yes, this is okay for me.
Great, Wednesday at 1. Can I get your name please?
My name is Linh Tran. L-I-N-H, last name T-R-A-N.
Linh Tran, got it. And your phone number?
Alright Linh, you're all set for Wednesday at 1 PM for that clogged kitchen drain — our plumber will call you before they come.
Okay thank you very much. They call before, yes?
Yes, about 30 minutes before — you'll get a call. Have a great day, Linh!
Smith Plumbing, this is Katie.
Hi yeah I need someone to fix a leak, what's your earliest availability?
Sure thing — where's the leak, under a sink, behind a wall?
Under the bathroom sink. It's dripping onto the floor. I've got a bucket there now but I need it fixed.
Bathroom sink leak with a bucket under it — got it. Let me pull up the schedule real quick.
Yeah, soon as possible please.
I've got tomorrow morning at 8 AM — would that work?
8 AM tomorrow, done. What do you need from me?
Just your name and a phone number.
Jason Reeves, 214-555-0822.
Jason Reeves, tomorrow at 8 AM for the bathroom sink leak — our tech will call you 30 minutes before.
Perfect, thanks.
You got it, Jason. Talk to you tomorrow!
Good morning, Smith Plumbing, this is Katie!
Oh hi Katie! So glad I got a real person. You know, I called another place and was on hold for twenty minutes — twenty minutes! Can you believe that?
Ha, well I'm glad you got through to us! What can I help you with today?

Right, right, so I've got this leak under my kitchen sink. My husband noticed it last night — he was getting a pot out of the cabinet and everything was soaked. And you know those cabinets are real wood, not that particle board stuff, so I'm worried about water damage.
Oh no, a leak under the kitchen sink soaking into your wood cabinets — yeah you definitely want to get that taken care of.

Exactly! And my husband, bless his heart, he tried to tighten something under there but I told him to leave it alone before he makes it worse. He's handy with some things but plumbing is not one of them, if you know what I mean.
Ha, smart move — let me check what we've got available for you.
Oh wonderful, yes please. And ideally a morning — I've got my book club on Wednesday afternoon and Thursday I'm watching my granddaughter.
I totally understand — unfortunately, I'm looking at the schedule and we're completely booked through next week.
Oh no, really? Wow. I mean, I guess everyone's having plumbing problems, huh? Must be the season or something.
It really has been busy! I can put you on our waitlist and we'll call the moment something opens up, or I can book you the first morning slot we have, which is Monday the 21st at 9 AM.
Monday the 21st... well, I guess that works. I'll just keep a big towel under there in the meantime. Should I turn the water off under the sink or anything?
Yes, if you can find the shutoff valves under the sink, turning those off is a great idea — it'll stop the dripping until we get there.
Okay I'll have my husband do that. He can handle a valve at least! So Monday the 21st, 9 AM. Do you need my info?
Yes please, just your name and phone number.
It's Pam Whitfield. W-H-I-T-F-I-E-L-D. And my number is 972-555-0614.
Pam Whitfield, Monday the 21st at 9 AM for that kitchen sink leak — and you're on the waitlist too in case something opens sooner.
Perfect, Katie, you've been so helpful. I'll tell my friends about you all!
Aw, that's so sweet, thank you Pam! We'll take great care of you. Have a wonderful day!
Smith Plumbing, this is Katie, how can I help?
Hi, um, I'm not really sure who to call for this but there's like... water? Coming from somewhere in my wall I think? Or maybe the ceiling?
Water coming from the wall or ceiling — can you tell if it's near a bathroom or kitchen on the other side?
Um, well the bathroom is upstairs and this is like in the living room ceiling downstairs. There's a wet spot and it's kind of, I don't know, brownish?
Okay, so a brown wet spot on the living room ceiling right below your upstairs bathroom — that really sounds like a pipe leak up in the ceiling.
Oh. Is that like... is that a big deal? I don't really know anything about this stuff.
It's definitely something you want looked at soon so it doesn't get worse, but our guys handle this kind of thing all the time — we'll take care of you.
Okay, yeah, I guess I should have someone come out then?
I'd recommend it. Let me check — I've got Tuesday at 11 AM or Wednesday at 3 PM, either of those work?
Uh, Tuesday I think? Wait, is that tomorrow? I'm sorry, I'm losing track of days.
Tuesday is the day after tomorrow — so two days from now, at 11 AM.
Yeah, okay, that works. Should I like... do anything in the meantime? Put a bucket or something?
A bucket underneath is a great idea, and if the spot is getting bigger, definitely give us a call back right away. Can I get your name?
Oh right, sorry. It's Megan Cho.
No worries, Megan! And a phone number?
Alright Megan, you're set for Tuesday at 11 AM for that ceiling leak — our plumber will call you about 30 minutes before.
Okay great, thank you so much. I feel better now.
Of course! Don't hesitate to call if anything changes before then. Have a good one, Megan!
Thanks for calling Smith Plumbing, this is Katie.
Yeah, my water heater is completely dead. No hot water at all. I've got three kids who need baths and I'm about to lose it.
Oh no, no hot water with three kids — I can only imagine. Is it a gas or electric water heater, do you know?
It's gas. And before you ask, yes the pilot light is out and I already tried relighting it three times. It won't stay lit.
Gas water heater, pilot light won't stay lit even after relighting — that's usually the thermocouple, our guys see that a lot.
Great, well can someone come out today? Or am I gonna be boiling water on the stove for a week?
Let me check — I don't have anything today, but I can get someone out there tomorrow morning at 9 AM.
Tomorrow morning. Fine. That's the best you can do?
That's the earliest I've got right now, I'm sorry — I know it's tough.
Alright, whatever, book it.
Done. Can I get your name?
Brian Kowalski. K-O-W-A-L-S-K-I.
And a phone number, Brian?
Brian Kowalski, tomorrow at 9 AM for the water heater — our tech will call before heading out.
Fine. Thanks.
Hang in there, Brian, we'll get your hot water back. Talk to you tomorrow!
Good morning, Smith Plumbing, this is Katie!
Hello? Yes, hello. I need some help with my hot water. It's not getting hot anymore, you see.
Your hot water isn't getting hot — when did that start happening?
Well, I think it was yesterday morning. I went to take my shower and it was just... lukewarm. And then by last night, nothing. Just cold.
So it went from lukewarm yesterday to completely cold — that sounds like your water heater needs attention.
That's what I figured. My late husband used to handle all this, but I don't know the first thing about it. Do you know, is it gas or electric — how do I tell?
If there's a flame or pilot light at the bottom, it's gas — if it just has a cord plugged into the wall, it's electric. But don't worry, our tech will figure all that out when they come.
Oh good, good. Can someone come this week? I don't want to be without hot water too long, you know, at my age cold showers aren't fun.
Of course not! Let me see — I've got Thursday at 10 AM, would that work for you?
Thursday at ten... yes, I believe that works. I don't have anything Thursday. Let me just check my calendar here... no, Thursday is clear.
Wonderful. Can I get your name, please?
It's Ruth Ann Pemberton. That's P-E-M-B-E-R-T-O-N.
Ruth Ann Pemberton, lovely. And a phone number to reach you?
My number is 940-555-0132. Would you like me to repeat that?
940-555-0132, I got it! So Ruth Ann, you're booked for Thursday at 10 AM for the water heater — we'll call you about 30 minutes before.
Oh wonderful, thank you so much, Katie. You've been just lovely.
Aw, thank you, Ruth Ann! We'll get that hot water running for you. Take care now!
Smith Plumbing, this is Katie, how can I help you?
Hello, yes, I have the, uh, how you say... the hot water machine? It is not working.
Your water heater isn't working — is the water coming out cold or not at all?
Cold. Very cold. Before it was okay, now only cold water. Maybe two day now.
Cold water for two days — I'm sorry about that. Let me see when we can get someone out to look at your water heater.
Yes please, when someone can come?
I'm really sorry, but we're fully booked this week and next week — the earliest I have is the 22nd.
The twenty-two? That is, uh, very long time. No hot water is very difficult.
I completely understand, two weeks without hot water is really tough. I can put you on our waitlist and call you the moment anything opens up sooner.
Okay, yes, the waitlist and also the twenty-two, yes? Both?
Yes, both — waitlist and booked for the 22nd at 11 AM. Can I get your name?
My name is Andrei Popescu. A-N-D-R-E-I, last name P-O-P-E-S-C-U.
Andrei Popescu, got it. And your phone number?
Alright Andrei, you're on the waitlist and booked for the 22nd at 11 AM for the water heater — we'll call you if something opens up sooner.
Thank you, I hope sooner. Thank you very much.
We'll do our best, Andrei. Have a good day!
Smith Plumbing, this is Katie.
Hey, I need someone to look at a toilet. It's running nonstop and driving up my water bill.
A toilet that won't stop running — yeah that'll definitely eat up your water bill. Is it in the main bathroom?
Master bath. What do you have open?
Let me check — I've got Friday at 1 PM, does that work?
Friday at one is fine. Name's Craig Bellamy, 469-555-0733.
Craig Bellamy, Friday at 1 PM, running toilet in the master bath — you're all set.
Great, thanks.
You got it, Craig. Our tech will call before they head out. Bye now!
Good afternoon, Smith Plumbing, this is Katie!

Hi Katie! Oh good, I was hoping to get someone friendly. So here's the deal — my toilet is doing this thing where it like, flushes on its own? In the middle of the night! Scared me half to death the first time, I thought there was a ghost in my bathroom.
Ha! A ghost flush — that's actually called phantom flushing and it means the flapper valve is probably leaking.

Phantom flushing! That's hilarious. I'm going to tell my friend Diane that, she'll get a kick out of it. She actually has the same problem but she just jiggles the handle. Anyway, it's been going on for maybe a month now and I finally decided to deal with it.
Well glad you called! A month of phantom flushing means you've probably been wasting a good bit of water too.
Oh I bet! My water bill did seem higher last month, actually. My husband said it was because of the garden but I'm like, no, it's the toilet, I keep telling you! Anyway, can you guys come fix it?
Absolutely, let me see what we've got. How about Wednesday at 3 PM?
Wednesday at three — oh wait, is that when Dr. Phil is on? Just kidding, I DVR everything now. Yes, Wednesday at three works perfectly!
Ha! Alright, Wednesday at 3 it is. Can I get your name and number?
Sure! It's Nancy Giordano. G-I-O-R-D-A-N-O. And my number, let me think, it's 214-555-0295. That's my cell, my landline is — actually no, just use the cell.
Nancy Giordano, 214-555-0295, Wednesday at 3 PM for the phantom flushing toilet — we'll give you a call 30 minutes before.
Perfect! And tell your plumber the ghost story, maybe they'll get a laugh. Thanks so much, Katie!
I absolutely will! Thanks for the laugh, Nancy. See you Wednesday!
Smith Plumbing, this is Katie, how can I help?
Look, I've got a toilet that's overflowing every time I flush it and I've already spent sixty bucks on a plunger and some snake thing from Home Depot that did absolutely nothing.
Ugh, an overflowing toilet after you've already tried to fix it yourself — that's so frustrating.
You're telling me. So what do you guys charge for something like this?
For a toilet clog like that, the service call is $89 and then the repair is usually between $150 and $250 depending on what we find.
Are you kidding me? Up to $250 on top of the $60 I already wasted? You know what, forget it, I'll figure it out myself.
I totally understand, that adds up. If you change your mind we're here — and I'll be honest, once a snake doesn't work it usually means the clog is deeper in the line, which needs a powered auger.
A powered... what, I can't rent one of those?
You can, but they can be tricky to use and if the clog is past the toilet flange, you risk cracking the porcelain — which would be a much bigger repair.
... Yeah, okay, that's a good point. Last thing I need is to crack the toilet. Fine, let's just do it. When can you come?
Sure thing — I've got Thursday at 2 PM, does that work?
Yeah, fine. Thursday at 2.
Great. Can I get your name and number?
Tony Marquez. 972-555-0861.
Tony Marquez, Thursday at 2 PM for the overflowing toilet — our tech will call you 30 minutes before.
Alright. This better fix it.
We'll get it sorted, Tony. See you Thursday!
Smith Plumbing, this is Katie!

Hi Katie! So I have kind of a random question. My wife and I just bought this gorgeous new kitchen faucet from a home show — one of those fancy ones with the pull-down sprayer, you know? And I'm wondering if you guys install those or if that's more of a handyman thing.
Oh nice! Yeah, we absolutely install kitchen faucets — that's right up our alley.
Perfect! So here's my other question — we already have the faucet, it's sitting in the box in the garage. Do you guys charge less if we supply the parts? Or is it the same either way?
Great question — yes, since you've already got the faucet, you'd just be paying the labor, which is our $89 service call plus usually about $125 to $175 for the install depending on the setup.

Oh that's not bad at all! Honestly, I was expecting more. My buddy Steve paid like $400 at some other place but I think they ripped him off. Do you guys handle the old faucet removal too, or do I need to take that off first?
We handle everything — removal of the old one, installing the new one, hooking up the water lines, and making sure there are no leaks.
Awesome. And one more thing — the hot water supply valve under the sink is a little corroded. Would the plumber be able to look at that while they're there?
Absolutely, they can check that valve and replace it if needed — we'd just add the cost of the valve which is usually around $20 to $30.
That's great to know. Alright, I think I have enough info — let me talk to the wife about timing and I'll call back to schedule. Thanks a ton, Katie!
Sounds good! Give us a call whenever you're ready and we'll get you on the schedule. Have a great day!
Good morning, Smith Plumbing, this is Katie.
Hi um, I'm not sure if this is the right place to call. My kitchen sink has this, like, the handle thing is broken? And water kind of sprays out the side when I turn it on.
Water spraying out the side of the faucet when you turn the handle — yeah, that's definitely us! Sounds like the faucet needs to be repaired or possibly replaced.
Oh okay. I don't know if it can be fixed or if I need a whole new... thing. Is that something your person would figure out when they come?
Exactly — our plumber will take a look and let you know if it just needs a part or if a new faucet makes more sense.
Okay, that makes me feel better. I was worried I'd have to pick one out first or something and I have no idea what I'm doing with that.
Nope, no homework required! Want me to get you on the schedule?
Um, yeah, I think so. What days do you have?
I've got Monday at 10 AM or Tuesday at 2 PM — which sounds better?
I think... Monday? Yeah, Monday morning. 10 is fine.
Monday at 10 AM it is. Can I get your name?
It's Sarah Nguyen. N-G-U-Y-E-N.
And a phone number, Sarah?
Sarah Nguyen, Monday at 10 AM for the kitchen faucet — our plumber will call you about 30 minutes before.
Okay perfect. Thank you for being so patient with me!
Of course, that's what we're here for! See you Monday, Sarah.
Smith Plumbing, this is Katie.
Hey, I think I've got a sewer line problem. Multiple drains backing up at the same time, sewage smell in the yard. Need someone out ASAP.
Multiple drains backing up with a sewage smell outside — that does sound like a main sewer line issue. Let me see what we've got.
What's your earliest?
I can get someone out there tomorrow morning at 8 AM.
Tomorrow at 8, that works. Name's Derek Shaw, 214-555-0618.
Derek Shaw, tomorrow at 8 AM for the sewer line — our tech will bring a camera to scope the line and figure out what's going on.
Good. Is there anything I should avoid doing tonight? Like running water or flushing?
Yeah, try to limit water usage as much as you can tonight — less going down the drains means less backup risk.
Got it. See you tomorrow.
See you then, Derek. Hang tight!
Smith Plumbing, this is Katie, how can I help you?
Oh thank goodness someone answered. I'm having a terrible problem. There's, um, sewage — I think it's sewage — coming up in my bathtub. And the smell, oh my, it's just awful.
Oh no, sewage coming up in your bathtub — that is an emergency. Is it actively rising right now?
Well it's not rising at the moment, but every time I run any water in the house — the kitchen, the bathroom — more comes up. I'm afraid to use anything.
You're doing the right thing by not running any water. This sounds like a main sewer line backup and we need to get someone out there right away.
Right away? Oh that would be wonderful. I'm 78 years old and I can't — I just can't live with this smell. It's making me nauseous.
I completely understand. Let me get our emergency tech dispatched to you — they can be there within about two hours. Can I get your name and number?
Yes, it's Evelyn Price. P-R-I-C-E. My phone number is, let me see, it's 817-555-0327.
Evelyn Price, 817-555-0327. And what's your address?
1408 Maple Creek Drive. That's in Arlington.
Got it. Evelyn, our emergency tech is being dispatched to 1408 Maple Creek Drive — they'll call you before they arrive, and in the meantime, please don't run any water at all.
I won't, I won't. And should I open a window or anything for the smell?
Yes, open some windows if you can, and if you have a fan, point it toward the bathroom to push the air out. We'll have someone there soon, Evelyn.
Thank you so much, dear. You've been so kind.
Of course, Evelyn. We're going to take care of this for you. Sit tight!
Smith Plumbing, this is Katie.
Hi, quick question — my garbage disposal just stopped working. It hums but doesn't spin. Do I need a plumber for that?
It hums but doesn't spin — that usually means something's jammed in there. Have you tried the reset button on the bottom of the unit?
There's a reset button?

Yep! There's a small red button on the bottom of the disposal — press that first. If it still just hums, there should be a hex wrench slot on the bottom too. Stick an Allen wrench in there and turn it back and forth to free whatever's stuck.
Allen wrench, bottom of the disposal, turn it back and forth. Got it.
That's it — make sure the power is off first before you stick anything in there, though!
Yeah yeah, power off first. And if that doesn't work?
Then give us a call back and we'll send someone out — but nine times out of ten that fixes it.
Alright cool, I'll try it now. Thanks.
Good luck! Call us if you need us.
Good afternoon, Smith Plumbing, this is Katie!

Hey Katie! So, funny story — I was making my famous Thanksgiving-style stuffing last night, don't ask me why in April, I just had a craving. Anyway, I may have put a little too much down the garbage disposal and now it's completely dead.
Ha! Stuffing took down the disposal — those starchy foods can really gum things up.

Right?! My wife told me not to put it all down there at once but did I listen? Of course not. I tried the little reset button thing on the bottom and the Allen wrench trick — nothing. I think I killed it.
If the reset and Allen wrench didn't do it, it might need a new motor or a full replacement — let me get someone out to take a look.
Yeah, my wife says if I don't get it fixed by the weekend she's making me wash dishes by hand for a month. So, you know, no pressure or anything.
Ha, well let's save you from dish duty! I've got Thursday at 11 AM — that'll be before the weekend.
Thursday at 11, perfect. I owe you one, Katie. Actually, I owe my wife one. She always says I should call the professionals first instead of trying to YouTube everything.
Your wife sounds like a smart woman! Can I get your name and number?
Ha, don't tell her that, she'll never let me forget it. It's Greg Patterson, 903-555-0215.
Greg Patterson, Thursday at 11 AM for the garbage disposal — we'll call you 30 minutes before.
Awesome. And hey, do your guys handle the replacement if it needs one, or do I need to buy a new disposal first?
We carry the most common models on the truck, so if it needs replacing, our tech can usually do it right then and there.
Oh that's great. Alright Katie, you've been a lifesaver. Or at least a dish-duty-saver. Thanks!
Ha! Good luck with the wife, Greg. See you Thursday!
Smith Plumbing, this is Katie.
I've got a burst pipe in my basement. Water is everywhere. I need someone NOW.
A burst pipe with water flooding — do you know where your main water shutoff valve is?
I already shut it off but there's still water all over the floor. It's soaking into everything.
Good, you got the water shut off — that's the most important thing. I'm dispatching our emergency tech to you right now. What's your name and address?
Kevin Torres, 2215 Oak Hill Lane, Mansfield. How fast can someone get here?
Our emergency tech can be there within about an hour. What's your phone number, Kevin?
That's the fastest we can get there — they're heading your way as soon as I hang up. In the meantime, if you have a wet vac or towels, start getting that standing water up to minimize damage.
Yeah I'll grab towels. Just get someone here.
They're on their way, Kevin. They'll call you when they're close. Hang in there!
Smith Plumbing, this is Katie, how can I help?

Yeah you can help by sending someone to my house right now! I've got water pouring out from under my bathroom wall and it's flooding into my hallway. This is a rental and my landlord won't pick up the phone!
Water pouring from the wall into the hallway — that's an emergency. First thing, can you find the main water shutoff and turn it off?
I don't even know where that is! I'm a renter, nobody showed me where anything is in this place!
Okay, check near your water heater or where the water line comes into the house — it's usually a round handle or lever on a pipe near the ground.
I — okay, hold on. I'm in the garage... I think I see it. Yeah there's a valve here. Okay I turned it. Water stopped.
Oh good, the water's stopped — great job. Now I'm getting our emergency tech headed your way right now. What's your name and address?
Tiffany Rowe, 504 Birchwood Court, unit B. In Euless.
And your phone number, Tiffany?
682-555-0741. How long is this gonna take? I've got water all over my floors, my stuff is getting ruined!
Our emergency tech can be there within about an hour and a half — I know that feels like forever right now, but they're coming as fast as they can.
An hour and a half! Are you serious right now?
I know, I'm sorry — they're already out on another emergency but you're next. In the meantime, get towels down on that water and take photos of everything for your landlord.
Oh trust me, I'm documenting everything. My landlord is going to hear about this.
That's smart. Our tech will call you when they're on the way — Tiffany Rowe, 504 Birchwood Court unit B, we've got you.
Okay. Thank you. Sorry for yelling, I'm just... this is a lot.
Don't even worry about it, Tiffany — this is stressful. We'll get it fixed for you. Hang in there!
